title: A Leave-Taking.
content:
She will not smile;
        She will not stir;
     I marvel while
        I look on her.
         The lips are chilly
            And will not speak;
         The ghost of a lily
            In either cheek.

     Her hair - ah me!
        Her hair - her hair!
     How helplessly
        My hands go there!
         But my caresses
            Meet not hers,
         O golden tresses
            That thread my tears!

     I kiss the eyes
        On either lid,
     Where her love lies
        Forever hid.
         I cease my weeping
            And smile and say:
         I will be sleeping
            Thus, some day!
meaning:
James Whitcomb Riley's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "A Leave-Taking."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
